entities.name: Graph Retrieval-Augmented Generation
entities.description: A process of building a graph retriever that involves multiple steps from understanding the problem domain to defining an ontology.

entities.name: Problem Domain
entities.description: The specific area or field for which the graph retriever is being built, determining the kind of reasoning the system should perform.

entities.name: Entities
entities.description: Important objects, concepts, or actors identified in the data that form the nodes of the graph.

entities.name: Relationships
entities.description: Interactions, dependencies, or causal links between entities that form the edges of the graph.

entities.name: Raw Data
entities.description: Unstructured and inconsistent information collected from various sources such as PDFs, manuals, APIs, databases, telemetry streams, maintenance records, schedules, logs, or web documents.

entities.name: Data Cleaning and Normalization
entities.description: The stage where terminology is standardized, duplicates are removed, formats are aligned, and inconsistencies are resolved to create coherent data.

entities.name: Chunking
entities.description: Dividing large documents into smaller chunks that can become evidence nodes or semantic references linked to entities.

entities.name: Entity Extraction
entities.description: Identifying important objects, concepts, or actors in the data using NLP models, rule-based systems, or LLM-based extraction pipelines.

entities.name: Relationship Extraction
entities.description: Analyzing text to discover interactions, dependencies, or causal links between entities.

entities.name: Ontology or Schema
entities.description: The structural blueprint of the graph that defines how entities and relationships are organized.

entities.name: flight
entities.description: An entity representing a connection between two airports in a travel system.

entities.name: airport
entities.description: A location node in the travel system graph.

entities.name: airline
entities.description: An entity that operates routes between airports.

entities.name: ontology
entities.description: A structural blueprint of the graph specifying allowed node types, relationships, and domain organization.

entities.name: graph
entities.description: A connected knowledge structure where entities are nodes and relationships are edges.

entities.name: graph database
entities.description: Databases optimized for relationship-based querying and traversal, e.g., Neo4j, Amazon Neptune, TigerGraph.

entities.name: embedding
entities.description: Vector representations generated for nodes, document chunks, summaries, or other graph elements to enable semantic similarity search.

entities.name: indexing
entities.description: The process of creating vector, graph, and metadata indexes to support semantic search, traversal, and filtering.

entities.name: retriever
entities.description: A hybrid retrieval system combining vector search, graph traversal, keyword matching, and metadata filtering.

entities.name: query processing
entities.description: The online phase where the system interprets user queries by extracting intent, entities, goals, constraints, and context.

entities.name: inverter
entities.description: An electrical device that converts DC to AC, subject to temperature changes and faults.

entities.name: temperature
entities.description: A measurable physical quantity indicating heat level, used to detect faults such as overheating.

entities.name: fault diagnosis
entities.description: The method of identifying and analyzing faults in systems like inverters using graph traversal and reasoning.

entities.name: graph traversal
entities.description: The method of exploring connected nodes and relationships in a graph to discover contextual reasoning paths.

entities.name: graph index
entities.description: An indexing structure that helps efficiently traverse relationships between nodes and edges in a graph.

entities.name: vector index
entities.description: An indexing method used to retrieve information based on similarity in vector space.

entities.name: metadata index
entities.description: An indexing method that organizes data based on metadata attributes to support retrieval.

entities.name: retriever
entities.description: A system module that searches for suitable starting nodes and performs graph traversal to extract relevant subgraphs.

entities.name: subgraph
entities.description: A relevant subset of nodes, relationships, and evidence extracted from the graph for contextual reasoning.

entities.name: LLM
entities.description: Large Language Model that generates responses using structured prompts derived from graph traversal.

entities.name: RAG system
entities.description: Retrieval-Augmented Generation system that supports adding additional knowledge and relationships dynamically.

entities.name: Graph Retrieval-Augmented Generation (Graph RAG) system
entities.description: A system architecture that combines graph, vector, and metadata indexes to enhance information retrieval and reasoning.

entities.name: Graph index
entities.description: An index that helps efficiently traverse relationships between nodes and edges in a graph.

entities.name: Vector index
entities.description: An index that supports semantic similarity search using embeddings.

entities.name: Metadata index
entities.description: An index that enables filtering and structured querying based on attributes such as time, severity, region, device type, confidence score, or document source.

entities.name: Semantic vector retrieval
entities.description: A retrieval method that uses vector indexes to identify candidate entities or chunks based on semantic similarity.

entities.name: Graph traversal engine
entities.description: A component that explores relationships around identified entities in a graph.

entities.name: Metadata filtering
entities.description: A method that narrows retrieved context based on operational constraints using metadata indexes.

entities.name: Graph RAG
entities.description: A graph-based Retrieval-Augmented Generation system that continuously ingests new documents and data, extracts entities and relationships, and updates the graph structure incrementally to maintain a living contextual memory.

entities.name: Entity
entities.description: Nodes in the graph representing concepts, components, or faults extracted from ingested data.

entities.name: Relationship
entities.description: Edges in the graph representing causal or associative links between entities.

entities.name: Embedding
entities.description: Vector representations of nodes and chunks used for semantic similarity search within the graph.

entities.name: Ingestion Pipeline
entities.description: The process that cleans, chunks, extracts entities and relationships from new data, compares with existing graph, and updates the graph accordingly.

entities.name: Predictive Maintenance Graph
entities.description: A graph containing nodes such as inverter, cooling system, MOSFETs, and fault entities used to model maintenance knowledge and failure modes.

entities.name: Fault Entity
entities.description: A node representing a newly observed failure mode or fault condition.

entities.name: Causal Relationship
entities.description: A directed edge indicating cause-effect linkage between entities.

entities.name: Chunk Nodes
entities.description: Additional nodes attached to entities or relationships providing evidence or context.

entities.name: Vector Index
entities.description: An index storing embeddings for efficient semantic similarity search.

entities.name: Streaming Updates
entities.description: The ability of the graph to incorporate new data in near real-time, such as sensor events or alerts.

entities.name: Domains
entities.description: Fields where Graph RAG is especially powerful, including predictive maintenance, healthcare, cybersecurity, supply chains, intelligent transportation, and digital twins.

entities.name: provenance
entities.description: Information about where a graph relationship came from, how it was created, and how trustworthy it is; the traceable origin and history of a node or edge.

entities.name: synchronous operation
entities.description: An operation where one operation directly waits for another operation to finish before continuing; the caller is blocked until the callee finishes.

entities.name: synchronous workflow
entities.description: Operations where the request lifecycle, execution ordering, and transactional consistency require the system to wait for deterministic completion before proceeding to the next stage.

entities.name: reconciliation worker
entities.description: A background service that checks whether different systems ended up in the same correct state by periodically comparing records and fixing or escalating mismatches; responsible for state correction and consistency checking.

entities.name: delayed retry
entities.description: A process that tries a failed operation again after a delay, potentially multiple times, or moves the failed operation to a dead-letter queue if it continues to fail.

entities.name: Apache Kafka
entities.description: A distributed event streaming platform used for building real-time data pipelines and streaming applications.

entities.name: Azure Event Hubs
entities.description: A big data streaming platform and event ingestion service capable of receiving and processing millions of events per second.

entities.name: Amazon Kinesis
entities.description: A platform on AWS to collect, process, and analyze real-time streaming data.

entities.name: cloud service buses
entities.description: Messaging infrastructure that allows different applications and services to communicate with each other asynchronously.

entities.name: Kafka
entities.description: An event streaming platform suitable for handling many continuous events and allowing multiple consumers to read the same event stream independently.

entities.name: Event Hubs
entities.description: An event streaming platform similar to Kafka, used for high-volume continuous events, analytics, monitoring streams, or many independent consumers.

entities.name: Kinesis
entities.description: An event streaming platform similar to Kafka and Event Hubs, used for continuous event streaming and multiple independent consumers.

entities.name: Azure Service Bus
entities.description: A cloud service bus used mainly for reliable task execution, retries, background jobs, and worker processing.

entities.name: AWS SQS
entities.description: A cloud service queue used for reliable task execution, retries, background jobs, and worker processing.

entities.name: RabbitMQ
entities.description: A message queue system used for reliable task execution, retries, background jobs, and worker processing.

entities.name: Event Streaming
entities.description: Publishing facts/events for many services to react to, suitable for continuous event flows and multiple independent consumers.

entities.name: Queue / Service Bus
entities.description: Assigning work/tasks for workers to process, suitable for reliable task execution, retries, and background jobs.

entities.name: Synchronous Execution
entities.description: Execution where the workflow waits for an operation to finish before continuing.

entities.name: Asynchronous Execution
entities.description: Execution where the workflow continues immediately without waiting for the operation to finish; results arrive later.

entities.name: Stateful
entities.description: System behaviour where workflow or session memory is stored between requests.

entities.name: Stateless
entities.description: System behaviour where no workflow or session memory is stored between requests.

entities.name: Stateful system
entities.description: A system that remembers previous context or workflow state, preserving information across requests.

entities.name: Stateless system
entities.description: A system that does not remember anything between requests; each request is independent.

entities.name: Redis
entities.description: A storage system commonly used to preserve workflow state or session memory in stateful systems.

entities.name: PostgreSQL
entities.description: A relational database system used to store workflow state or session memory.

entities.name: Session store
entities.description: A storage mechanism used to preserve session information in stateful systems.

entities.name: Workflow engine
entities.description: A system component that manages and preserves workflow state.

entities.name: Synchronous execution
entities.description: Execution coordination where processes wait for each other to complete.

entities.name: Asynchronous execution
entities.description: Execution coordination where processes do not wait for each other to complete.

entities.name: Graph Retriever pipeline
entities.description: A system for retrieval-augmented generation using graph-based data.

entities.name: Offline Graph Construction Phase
entities.description: Phase involving building the knowledge graph before query time.

entities.name: Online Retrieval & Traversal Phase
entities.description: Phase involving query-time reasoning and retrieval from the graph.

entities.name: Raw Data
entities.description: Initial unprocessed data input for the graph retriever pipeline.

entities.name: Ingestion
entities.description: Process of importing raw data into the system.

entities.name: Chunking & Cleaning
entities.description: Process of dividing data into manageable pieces and cleaning it.

entities.name: Entity Extraction
entities.description: Process of identifying entities from the data.

entities.name: Relationship Extraction
entities.description: Process of identifying relationships between entities.

entities.name: Ontology / Schema Design
entities.description: Designing the schema or ontology for the knowledge graph.

entities.name: Graph Construction
entities.description: Building the knowledge graph from extracted entities and relationships.

entities.name: Graph Storage & Indexing
entities.description: Storing and indexing the graph for efficient retrieval.

entities.name: Embedding Generation
entities.description: Generating vector embeddings for graph elements to support retrieval.

entities.name: Hybrid Retrieval Layer
entities.description: Layer combining different retrieval methods for querying the graph.

entities.name: Graph Traversal Engine
entities.description: Engine that performs traversal operations on the graph during queries.

entities.name: Subgraph Assembly
entities.description: Assembling relevant subgraphs for answering queries.

entities.name: Prompt Construction
entities.description: Constructing prompts for large language model reasoning.

entities.name: LLM Reasoning
entities.description: Reasoning performed by large language models using graph data.

entities.name: Final Response
entities.description: The final answer generated by the system.

entities.name: Domain
entities.description: The application area for the graph retriever, e.g., flight booking.

entities.name: Objective
entities.description: The goal of the graph retriever, e.g., route reasoning.

entities.name: Node types
entities.description: Types of nodes in the graph, e.g., airport, airline, flight.

entities.name: Edge types
entities.description: Types of edges in the graph, e.g., connects_to, operated_by.

entities.name: Queries
entities.description: Example queries the system should answer, e.g., cheapest route avoiding overnight stops.

entities.name: Raw Data
entities.description: Initial collected data from various sources such as PDFs, APIs, databases, logs, tables, and web data.

entities.name: PDFs
entities.description: Manuals provided in PDF format used as a source of raw data.

entities.name: APIs
entities.description: Flight data accessed through APIs used as a source of raw data.

entities.name: Databases
entities.description: Maintenance records stored in databases used as a source of raw data.

entities.name: Logs
entities.description: Sensor logs used as a source of raw data.

entities.name: Tables
entities.description: Schedules stored in tables used as a source of raw data.

entities.name: Web Data
entities.description: Policies and other relevant data collected from the web.

entities.name: Cleaning & Normalization
entities.description: Phase involving preparation of data by removing duplicates, standardizing names, normalizing units, cleaning formatting, and resolving inconsistencies.

entities.name: Chunking
entities.description: Phase where large documents are split into smaller units such as sections, paragraphs, and chunks.

entities.name: Entity Extraction
entities.description: Phase where important entities are identified using NLP, NER models, LLM extraction, or rules/patterns.

entities.name: Graph RAG
entities.description: A graph-based retrieval augmented generation method that uses chunking to create evidence nodes and embeddings.

entities.name: NLP
entities.description: Natural Language Processing used for entity extraction.

entities.name: NER models
entities.description: Named Entity Recognition models used for identifying important entities.

entities.name: LLM extraction
entities.description: Large Language Model extraction techniques used for entity identification.

entities.name: MOSFET Q3
entities.description: A MOSFET transistor labeled Q3 in the power electronics system.

entities.name: coolant failure
entities.description: Failure of the cooling system leading to inadequate heat dissipation.

entities.name: MOSFET_Q3
entities.description: A MOSFET component in the power electronics system.

entities.name: Coolant_Failure
entities.description: A failure mode related to the cooling system that can affect components.

entities.name: Neo4j
entities.description: A popular graph database used for storing graph data.

entities.name: TigerGraph
entities.description: A high-scale graph database option.

entities.name: Amazon Neptune
entities.description: An AWS-native graph database service.

entities.name: ArangoDB
entities.description: A database that supports both graph and document data models.

entities.name: Graph RAG
entities.description: Modern retrieval augmented generation combining graph structure and vector embeddings.

entities.name: Vector Embeddings
entities.description: Vector representations generated for nodes, chunks, summaries, and sometimes edges to enable semantic search and reasoning.

entities.name: Hybrid Retrieval
entities.description: A retrieval approach combining vector, graph, keyword, and metadata indexes.

entities.name: inverter
entities.description: A power electronics device that converts DC to AC, subject to temperature variations.

entities.name: temperature
entities.description: A measurable physical quantity indicating the thermal state of a component.

entities.name: temperature sensor
entities.description: A device that measures the temperature of components such as the inverter.

entities.name: cooling system
entities.description: A system designed to manage and reduce the temperature of power electronics components.

entities.name: cooling loop
entities.description: Part of the cooling system responsible for circulating coolant to dissipate heat.

entities.name: pump
entities.description: A mechanical device that circulates coolant within the cooling loop.

entities.name: pump failure
entities.description: A malfunction of the pump leading to reduced coolant flow.

entities.name: reduced coolant flow
entities.description: A state where the coolant flow is insufficient, potentially causing overheating.

entities.name: MOSFET overheating
entities.description: A condition where the MOSFET device exceeds safe temperature limits, risking damage.

entities.name: MOSFET
entities.description: A type of transistor used for switching and amplifying electronic signals.

entities.name: sensor alerts
entities.description: Notifications generated by sensors to indicate abnormal conditions or faults.

entities.name: reranking
entities.description: A method to reorder nodes based on relevance or importance.

entities.name: scoring
entities.description: Assigning numerical values to nodes to evaluate their significance.

entities.name: relevance filtering
entities.description: Filtering out nodes that are not relevant to the current context.

entities.name: confidence weighting
entities.description: Assigning weights to nodes based on confidence levels.

entities.name: structured prompt
entities.description: A formatted input built from relevant components and reasoning paths for LLM processing.

entities.name: LLM reasoning
entities.description: Using a large language model to analyze query, context, and reasoning paths to generate responses.

entities.name: response generation
entities.description: Producing answers, explanations, recommendations, and reasoning traces from the LLM.

entities.name: feedback loop
entities.description: Capturing user feedback and updating the knowledge graph with new information.

entities.name: Offline Build Phase
entities.description: A phase involving data processing steps such as entity extraction, relationship extraction, ontology creation, graph building, embeddings generation, and indexing.

entities.name: Online Query Phase
entities.description: A phase involving user query processing steps including query understanding, start node detection, graph traversal, subgraph extraction, prompt building, LLM reasoning, and generating the final answer.

entities.name: Classical RAG
entities.description: A retrieval approach that retrieves similar chunks of data.

entities.name: Graph RAG
entities.description: A retrieval approach that retrieves connected reasoning structures.

relationships.description: The understanding of the problem domain determines what entities will exist inside the graph.
The understanding of the problem domain determines what relationships will exist inside the graph.
Raw data is cleaned and normalized to resolve inconsistencies and standardize information.
Cleaned and normalized data is divided into smaller chunks for easier reference and evidence linking.
Chunks serve as semantic references that help identify entities.
Entity extraction identifies important objects and concepts that become nodes in the graph.
Relationship extraction identifies connections between entities that form edges in the graph.
Entities are linked through relationships to form the graph network.
Entities are structured according to the defined ontology or schema.
Relationships are structured according to the defined ontology or schema.
A flight connects two airports forming edges in the travel system graph.
An airline operates the route represented by a flight.
The ontology defines the structure and organization of the graph.
Entities are represented as nodes in the graph.
Relationships between entities are represented as edges in the graph.
The graph is stored in specialized graph databases optimized for querying and traversal.
Embeddings allow the system to perform semantic similarity search in addition to structural traversal.

relationships.target: vector search, graph traversal, keyword matching, metadata filtering
relationships.description: The retriever integrates multiple search and filtering methods into a unified retrieval framework.
Indexing creates vector, graph, and metadata indexes to support various retrieval operations.

relationships.target: intent, entities, goals, constraints, contextual meaning
relationships.description: During query processing, the system extracts key components from the user query to understand it.
The inverter's temperature is monitored to detect faults such as overheating.
Fault diagnosis relies on graph traversal to explore connected nodes and relationships for reasoning.
The retriever moves through the graph to discover contextual reasoning paths.
Graph traversal extracts a relevant subgraph containing useful nodes and relationships for reasoning.
The extracted subgraph is transformed into a structured prompt for the LLM to generate responses.
The LLM produces explanations, recommendations, and diagnostic conclusions based on graph context.
Graph index enables efficient traversal of relationships between nodes and edges.
Vector index supports similarity-based retrieval of information.
Metadata index supports retrieval based on metadata attributes.
RAG architectures enable continuous updating of the graph with new knowledge and relationships.
Graph RAG systems use graph indexes to efficiently traverse relationships between nodes.
Graph RAG systems use vector indexes to support semantic similarity search.
Graph RAG systems use metadata indexes to enable filtering and structured querying.

relationships.target: Queries about connections and reasoning paths
relationships.description: Graph index answers questions like what is connected to an entity or what reasoning path exists between nodes.
Vector index answers questions about information semantically similar to a query.
Metadata index answers queries filtered by attributes such as time, severity, or device type.
Semantic vector retrieval identifies candidate entities or chunks based on semantic similarity.
Graph traversal engine explores relationships around entities identified by semantic vector retrieval.
Metadata filtering narrows the retrieved context based on operational constraints.

relationships.target: Graph growth after embedding and indexing
relationships.description: Graph RAG systems are designed to evolve and grow after the initial embedding and indexing stage.
Processes new data to extract entities and relationships and incrementally update the graph structure.
Represents concepts or components as nodes within the graph.
Represents links between entities as edges within the graph.
Provides vector representations for semantic similarity search.
Provides vector representations for evidence or context chunks.
New information can create new nodes if entities are previously unknown.
New information can create new edges or strengthen existing relationships.
Provide additional context or evidence to existing entities.
Provide additional context or evidence to existing relationships.
Can incorporate near real-time data such as sensor events or alerts to continuously evolve.
Used in various fields where evolving knowledge structures are beneficial.
An example application of Graph RAG modeling maintenance knowledge and failure modes.
Represents a failure mode node within the graph.
Represents cause-effect edges within the graph.
Provenance provides traceable origin and history information about graph relationships.
Synchronous operations follow the sequence where the caller waits for the callee to finish before continuing.
Synchronous workflows require the system to wait for deterministic completion before proceeding.
Reconciliation workers inspect final states across systems and repair inconsistencies.
Delayed retry mechanism attempts to repeat failed actions after a delay.
Reconciliation workers inspect and repair state inconsistencies, whereas delayed retries simply repeat failed operations.
Retries may eventually succeed in completing the operation.
Reconciliation workers check whether the entire workflow has become consistent after retries.
Both are event streaming platforms but do not perform exactly the same functions.
Both are event streaming platforms but do not perform exactly the same functions.
All are messaging or event streaming technologies but differ in capabilities and implementations.
Kafka is an example of an event streaming platform.
Event Hubs is an example of an event streaming platform.
Kinesis is an example of an event streaming platform.
Azure Service Bus is an example of a cloud service bus or queue.
AWS SQS is an example of a cloud service queue.
RabbitMQ is an example of a message queue system.
Event streaming platforms are used for handling high-volume continuous events.
Queues and service buses are used for reliable task execution, retries, and background jobs.
Synchronous execution involves waiting for an operation to complete before continuing.

relationships.target: Not waiting for operation to finish
relationships.description: Asynchronous execution involves continuing immediately without waiting for operation completion.
Stateful systems store memory between requests.
Stateless systems do not store memory between requests.
SYNC actions are similar to stateful behaviour as they involve waiting for another action before proceeding.
Stateful systems often store workflow state in Redis.
Stateful systems often store workflow state in PostgreSQL.
Stateful systems often use session stores to preserve session memory.
Stateful systems use workflow engines to manage and preserve workflow state.
Synchronous and asynchronous describe different execution coordination and waiting behaviors.
Stateful and stateless describe different persistence of workflow or session memory.
Synchronous/asynchronous and stateful/stateless represent different architectural dimensions.
Raw data is ingested into the system.
Ingested data is chunked and cleaned.
Cleaned data is used for entity extraction.
Extracted entities are used to find relationships.
Extracted relationships inform ontology or schema design.
Ontology/schema guides graph construction.
Constructed graph is stored and indexed.
Stored graph is used to generate embeddings.
Embeddings support hybrid retrieval.
Hybrid retrieval results are processed by the traversal engine.
Traversal engine assembles relevant subgraphs.
Assembled subgraphs are used to construct prompts.
Prompts are used by large language models for reasoning.
LLM reasoning produces the final response.
The domain sets the context for the graph retriever.
The objective defines the goal of the graph retriever.
Node types define the nodes used in graph construction.
Edge types define the edges used in graph construction.
Queries are the inputs the graph retriever answers.
Raw data is collected from manuals in PDF format.
Raw data is collected from flight data accessed via APIs.
Raw data is collected from maintenance records stored in databases.
Raw data is collected from sensor logs.
Raw data is collected from schedules stored in tables.
Raw data is collected from policies and other data on the web.
Cleaning & Normalization prepares raw data by removing duplicates, standardizing names, normalizing units, cleaning formatting, and resolving inconsistencies.
Chunking splits large documents into smaller units such as sections, paragraphs, and chunks.
Graph RAG uses chunking to create evidence nodes and to assign embeddings.
Entity Extraction uses NLP techniques to identify important entities.
Entity Extraction uses Named Entity Recognition models to identify important entities.
Entity Extraction uses Large Language Model extraction techniques to identify important entities.
The MOSFET Q3 overheated as a result of the coolant failure.
Coolant failure can cause issues or failure in the MOSFET_Q3 component.
Neo4j is a common option for storing graph data.
TigerGraph is a high-scale option for graph storage.
Amazon Neptune is an AWS-native graph database used for graph storage.
ArangoDB supports graph and document storage.
Graph RAG methodology combines graph structure with vector embeddings.
Graph RAG methodology combines graph structure with vector embeddings.
Vector embeddings are generated for nodes to enable semantic search.
Vector embeddings are generated for chunks to enable retrieval.
Vector embeddings are generated for summaries to enable reasoning.
Vector embeddings are sometimes generated for edges to represent semantic relations.
Vector index is used for semantic retrieval.
Graph index is used for traversal.
Keyword index (BM25) is used for keyword-based retrieval.
Metadata index is used for filtering.
Hybrid retrieval combines vector index with other indexes.
Hybrid retrieval combines graph index with other indexes.
Hybrid retrieval combines keyword index with other indexes.
Hybrid retrieval combines metadata index with other indexes.
The inverter's temperature is monitored by a temperature sensor.
The inverter is cooled by the cooling system to maintain safe operating temperatures.
The cooling system includes the cooling loop as a subsystem.
The cooling loop's coolant is circulated by the pump.
The pump can fail, leading to malfunction.
Pump failure causes reduced coolant flow in the cooling loop.
Reduced coolant flow leads to MOSFET overheating.
MOSFET overheating negatively affects inverter operation.
Reranking is applied during context ranking and filtering to improve node usefulness.
Scoring is used to evaluate the importance of traversed nodes.
Relevance filtering removes non-useful nodes during context ranking.
Confidence weighting assigns weights to nodes based on certainty.
Structured prompts are built during prompt assembly phase.
LLM reasoning is conducted in phase 18 using query, context, and reasoning paths.
Response generation occurs in phase 19 producing answers and recommendations.
Feedback loop is optionally performed in phase 20 to update the graph.
Sensor alerts monitor the condition of MOSFET components.
Entity extraction is a step within the offline build phase.
Relationship extraction is a step within the offline build phase.
Ontology creation is a step within the offline build phase.
Graph building is a step within the offline build phase.
Embeddings generation is a step within the offline build phase.
Indexing is a step within the offline build phase.
Query understanding is a step within the online query phase.
Start node detection is a step within the online query phase.
Graph traversal is a step within the online query phase.
Subgraph extraction is a step within the online query phase.
Prompt building is a step within the online query phase.
LLM reasoning is a step within the online query phase.
Final answer generation is the last step within the online query phase.
Classical RAG retrieves similar chunks of data.
Graph RAG retrieves connected reasoning structures.
Graph RAG fundamentally differs from Classical RAG by retrieving connected reasoning structures instead of similar chunks.
